title: lactuca laevigata var. lamii
intro: Lactuca laevigata var. lamii, also known as Larkspur lettuce or Prickly lettuce, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that has been used for medicinal and culinary purposes. The plant contains lactucarium, a sesquiterpene lactone with sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which makes it useful for treating anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Lactuca laevigata var. lamii has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, bronchitis, and menstrual cramps, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, or used fresh in salads, to utilize its medicinal properties.
